page: Microsoft Azure E32s_v6 is an Esv6-family memory-optimized virtual machine utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor is equipped with 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and a large 260 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated mathematical operations, and cryptographic standards like AES and SHA_NI.

The instance is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, which aligns with memory-intensive workload requirements. While the server does not include local storage, it is Premium Storage capable, allowing high-performance external disk attachments. It does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile offers a high-density memory configuration relative to its compute resources, making it a cost-efficient choice for workloads that require large memory footprints without the overhead of local physical disks.

Benchmark data shows strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression at 30.93 GB/sec, Geekbench single-core execution at 2001.0, and static web serving throughput at 54.65 GB/sec. It also demonstrates top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models at 390.88 tokens/sec. Conversely, its PassMark memory score falls in the lower 25th percentile, indicating potential memory latency tradeoffs under specific synthetic workloads. Multi-core performance remains average, with a PassMark CPU score of 46370.85 and a multi-core Geekbench score of 15239.0. This server is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, static web serving, and lightweight LLM inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E32s_v6 is an Esv6-family memory-optimized virtual machine powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 32 vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz, backed by a 260 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The instance features 256.0 GB of RAM and lacks local storage, though it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core performance, static web serving throughput of 54.65 GB/sec, and top-tier text generation for small LLM models, though synthetic memory latency tests show some tradeoffs. This configuration is optimized for memory-heavy workloads, database operations, and web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32s_v6 is a memory-optimized VM with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, built for database and memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and 256 GB of RAM for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
256 GB of RAM providing an 8.0 GB per vCPU memory-to-core ratio
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated computing
Premium Storage capable configuration with no local disk storage
Strong performance in static web serving and single-threaded compression
Suited for database operations, memory-heavy applications, and web serving
